steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The passage says flying buttresses “eliminated” the need for thick walls. The blank introduces what happened next — Gothic cathedrals could use thinner, higher walls. That is a consequence, not a similarity, an example, or a contrast.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong transitions and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — Similarly,: “Similarly” signals that the next idea resembles the previous one, but thinner walls are a result of the invention, not a parallel example of it. ✗
  • Choice B — For instance,: “For instance” introduces an example that illustrates the previous statement, but Gothic cathedrals having thinner walls is not an illustration of the flying buttress invention — it is what the invention made possible. ✗
  • Choice C — Nevertheless,: “Nevertheless” signals contrast or concession, implying thinner walls happened despite the invention, which contradicts the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — As a result,: “As a result” directly signals that thinner, higher walls were a consequence of eliminating the need for thick walls — exactly the cause-effect relationship in the passage. ✓
That matches Choice D — As a result,.
